articles.title: WA sues 'just a bookie with a fancy name'
articles.summary: Washington state is suing a betting operator, dismissing it as nothing more than a traditional bookie disguised with modern branding. The case could set precedent for how regulators classify and prosecute prediction market platforms that blur the line between sophisticated forecasting and old-school gambling.

articles.title: $10 Kalshi Referral Code MASSLIVE1 extended for No. 3 Michigan State vs. No. 2 UConn
articles.summary: Kalshi is extending their $10 referral bonus code MASSLIVE1 to coincide with the high-profile March Madness matchup between No. 3 Michigan State and No. 2 UConn. The timing suggests prediction platforms are using marquee college basketball games to drive user acquisition during peak betting season when casual money floods the markets.

articles.title: Kalshi Approved for Margin Trading as It Lures Wall Street Pros
articles.summary: Kalshi just got regulatory approval to offer margin trading, letting traders leverage their bets on everything from elections to economic indicators. This puts them in direct competition with traditional Wall Street derivatives markets and signals that prediction markets are finally being taken seriously by institutional money.

articles.title: Sweet 16 Prediction Apps: Get Top Sign-Up Bonus for Kalshi, Novig, More
articles.summary: March Madness is driving prediction platforms like Kalshi and Novig to roll out their biggest sign-up bonuses of the year to capture basketball betting traffic. The tournament represents a crucial customer acquisition moment for newer prediction markets trying to steal market share from traditional sportsbooks when casual bettors are most engaged.

articles.title: Kalshi Sued in WA
articles.summary: Kalshi faces a lawsuit in Washington state, likely over regulatory compliance or market operations in the jurisdiction. This could signal broader state-level pushback against prediction markets, potentially forcing platforms to navigate a patchwork of conflicting regulations that could limit where Americans can legally bet on future events.

articles.title: California bans officials from using inside knowledge to bet on prediction markets
articles.summary: California just passed legislation prohibiting government officials from placing bets on prediction markets using non-public information they gain through their positions. The move signals that regulators are taking prediction markets seriously enough to worry about insider trading, which legitimizes the space while creating new compliance headaches for platforms.

articles.title: Insider trading concerns around oil and military moves are on the rise. Can anyone police the bets?
articles.summary: Traders are increasingly betting on oil prices and defense stocks ahead of major geopolitical events, raising red flags about potential insider information from government and military sources. Prediction markets face the same vulnerability since oil futures and conflict odds move on the same leaked intel that corrupts traditional securities markets.

articles.title: Prediction markets top $20 billion in monthly volume as geopolitics now drives majority of activity: TRM Labs
articles.summary: Prediction markets hit $20 billion in monthly volume with geopolitical events now accounting for most of the action, according to TRM Labs data. The shift from sports betting to political outcomes signals prediction markets are becoming serious financial instruments that rival traditional derivatives in liquidity and influence.

articles.title: NYSE owner doubles down on Polymarket with fresh $600 million investment
articles.summary: Intercontinental Exchange, which owns the New York Stock Exchange, just pumped $600 million into Polymarket in what appears to be a follow-up investment to their previous backing of the prediction platform. This massive bet signals that traditional financial giants see real money in prediction markets and validates Polymarket's model just as regulatory scrutiny heats up.

articles.title: Homophones, Plurals and Language ‘Rulescucks’ Are Roiling Prediction Markets
articles.summary: Prediction markets are getting tangled up in semantic disputes over word interpretation, with traders arguing about whether "homophones" count as separate answers and how plurals should be resolved. These linguistic loopholes are creating profitable arbitrage opportunities for grammar nerds while frustrating casual bettors who thought they were betting on substance, not English class technicalities.

articles.title: Israeli Air Force major charged with using classified info to place bets on Polymarket
articles.summary: An Israeli Air Force major has been charged with leveraging classified military intelligence to make profitable bets on Polymarket. The case highlights how insider information from military and government sources could corrupt prediction markets that rely on public information for accurate price discovery.

articles.title: A Valuation of Polymarket (POLY)
articles.summary: Polymarket's native token POLY is getting a formal valuation analysis, likely examining its utility within the platform's ecosystem and potential market value. This matters because POLY's price could signal broader institutional confidence in prediction markets and determine whether Polymarket can sustain its dominance as competitors like Kalshi and Manifold gain ground.
